items.title: Tongfu-Xingshen Capsule ameliorates ischemic stroke by inhibiting pyroptosis-mediated neuroinflammation
items.summary: Tongfu-Xingshen Capsule (TXC), a traditional Chinese medicine, is suggested to ameliorate ischemic stroke by inhibiting pyroptosis-mediated neuroinflammation, showing potential in mitigating stroke-associated pneumonia and preserving blood-brain barrier integrity.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究表明通腑醒神胶囊（TXC）可能通过抑制细胞焦亡介导的神经炎症来改善缺血性卒中。尽管是中药研究，了解其潜在机制有助于你关注中西医结合康复策略的未来发展。
items.titleZh: 通腑醒神胶囊通过抑制细胞焦亡介导的神经炎症改善缺血性卒中
items.summaryZh: 传统中药通腑醒神胶囊（TXC）被认为通过抑制细胞焦亡介导的神经炎症来改善缺血性卒中，显示出在减轻卒中相关肺炎和保护血脑屏障完整性方面的潜力。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study suggests that Tongfu-Xingshen Capsule (TXC) may ameliorate ischemic stroke by inhibiting pyroptosis-mediated neuroinflammation. Although it's a traditional Chinese medicine study, understanding its potential mechanisms can help you stay informed about future developments in integrated Western and Chinese medicine rehabilitation strategies.
items.limitation: 本研究的详细方法和结果未在摘要中充分披露，且为药物研究，其直接临床实践指导意义有限。
items.limitationEn: The detailed methods and results of this study are not fully disclosed in the abstract, and as a pharmaceutical study, its direct clinical practice guidance is limited.

items.title: A preliminary analysis of the inflammatory protein landscape in the CSF of mid- to late-stage Parkinson’s disease: associations with motor severity and subtypes | BMC Neurology | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: This preliminary analysis characterized inflammatory proteins in the cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) of mid- to late-stage Parkinson's disease (PD) patients and their associations with motor severity and subtypes, recognizing neuroinflammation as a contributor to clinical progression.
items.curatedReason: 这项初步分析探讨了中晚期帕金森病患者脑脊液中炎症蛋白与运动严重程度及亚型的关联。作为临床医师，它提示你神经炎症在 PD 进展中的作用，未来可能发展出更精确的生物标志物来指导个性化康复。
items.titleZh: 中晚期帕金森病脑脊液炎症蛋白图谱的初步分析：与运动严重程度和亚型的关联
items.summaryZh: 这项初步分析描述了中晚期帕金森病（PD）患者脑脊液（CSF）中的炎症蛋白，及其与运动严重程度和亚型的关联，认识到神经炎症是临床进展的促成因素。
items.curatedReasonEn: This preliminary analysis explores the association between inflammatory proteins in CSF and motor severity/subtypes in mid- to late-stage Parkinson's disease. As a clinician, this suggests the role of neuroinflammation in PD progression, potentially leading to more precise biomarkers for personalized rehabilitation in the future.
items.limitation: 本研究为初步分析，样本量和人群可能有限，且为观察性研究，不能建立因果关系。
items.limitationEn: This is a preliminary, observational study with potentially limited sample size and population, and it cannot establish causality.

items.title: Efficacy of remote ischemic conditioning on cerebral blood flow regulation in patients with endovascular stenting: a randomized controlled trial | BMC Medicine | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the efficacy of adjunctive remote ischemic conditioning (RIC) in improving cerebral blood flow (CBF) regulation and reducing postoperative stroke risk in patients undergoing endovascular stenting for cerebrovascular stenosis.
items.curatedReason: 这项 RCT 探讨了远程缺血预处理 (RIC) 对血管内支架植入术患者脑血流调节的疗效。这提示你在围手术期康复中，可关注 RIC 这类非侵入性干预对神经保护的潜在作用，辅助改善患者预后。
items.titleZh: 远程缺血预处理对血管内支架植入术患者脑血流调节的疗效：一项随机对照试验
items.summaryZh: 这项随机对照试验研究了辅助性远程缺血预处理（RIC）在接受脑血管狭窄血管内支架植入术的患者中，对改善脑血流（CBF）调节和降低术后卒中风险的疗效。
items.curatedReasonEn: This RCT investigates the efficacy of remote ischemic conditioning (RIC) on cerebral blood flow regulation in patients undergoing endovascular stenting. This suggests that you should consider the potential neuroprotective role of non-invasive interventions like RIC in perioperative rehabilitation to help improve patient outcomes.
items.limitation: 摘要未提及具体的样本量和主要结局指标，限制了对研究强度的判断。
items.limitationEn: The abstract does not mention the specific sample size and primary outcome measures, limiting the assessment of the study's strength.

items.title: Serum Biomarkers in Paediatric Neurosurgery: Traumatic Brain Injury and Beyond | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: This review discusses the role of serum biomarkers in pediatric neurosurgery, particularly in traumatic brain injury (TBI) and beyond, highlighting their potential for diagnosis, prognosis, and therapeutic monitoring.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述探讨了血清生物标志物在儿科神经外科（包括创伤性脑损伤）中的应用。作为物理治疗师，了解这些生物标志物的研究进展，有助于你更好地理解 TBI 等儿童神经损伤的病理生理学，并与医疗团队协作。
items.titleZh: 儿科神经外科中的血清生物标志物：创伤性脑损伤及其他
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述讨论了血清生物标志物在儿科神经外科中的作用，特别是创伤性脑损伤（TBI）及其他领域，强调了它们在诊断、预后和治疗监测方面的潜力。
items.curatedReasonEn: Serum biomarkers show great potential in pediatric neurosurgery, especially in the diagnosis and prognostic assessment of pediatric TBI. As a physical therapist, even if you don't directly use these markers, understanding them can help you better comprehend the extent of neurological injury and recovery potential in children, thereby optimizing your rehabilitation strategies and communicating effectively with the medical team.
items.limitation: 这是一篇综述，主要关注生物标志物在诊断和预后中的应用，不直接提供康复干预的证据。
items.limitationEn: This is a review, primarily focusing on the application of biomarkers in diagnosis and prognosis, and does not directly provide evidence for rehabilitation interventions.

items.title: Composite severity score for race-related incidents in cycling
items.summary: This paper proposes an incident-level severity framework for surveillance in mass-start cycling sports to provide deeper insights into race incident severity beyond traditional injury incidence measures.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究提出的自行车赛事故复合严重程度评分框架，为运动康复临床医师和研究者评估和分类自行车相关事件提供了新工具。这有助于更系统地理解损伤机制和预防策略，尤其是在集体出发、高速骑行背景下。
items.titleZh: 自行车赛中与竞赛相关的事故复合严重程度评分
items.summaryZh: 本文提出了一个用于集体出发自行车赛事故监测的严重程度框架，旨在提供超越传统损伤发生率测量的更深层事故严重程度洞察。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study's proposed composite severity score framework for race-related incidents in cycling offers a new tool for sports rehabilitation clinicians and researchers to assess and classify cycling events. This will aid in a more systematic understanding of injury mechanisms and prevention strategies, particularly in mass-start, high-speed cycling contexts.
items.limitation: 本研究仅提出了一个评分框架，未进行实际验证和广泛应用评估。
items.limitationEn: This study only proposed a scoring framework, without actual validation or extensive application assessment.

items.title: Ready to respond? Safeguarding structures in European National Olympic Committees
items.summary: A cross-sectional survey of 20 European National Olympic Committees (NOCs) assessed safeguarding structures and readiness to respond to interpersonal violence, identifying common challenges despite high officer appointment rates.
items.curatedReason: 这项针对欧洲各国奥委会（NOCs）的调查揭示了运动中人际暴力保障机制的现状和差距。作为运动康复领域的从业者，你应了解这些结构性问题，并思考如何在自己的实践中更好地识别、报告和支持受害者，确保运动员的安全。
items.titleZh: 准备好应对了吗？欧洲国家奥委会的保障结构
items.summaryZh: 一项针对 20 个欧洲国家奥委会（NOCs）的横断面调查评估了人际暴力保障结构和响应准备情况，尽管保障官员任命率高，但仍识别出常见挑战。
items.curatedReasonEn: This survey of European National Olympic Committees (NOCs) reveals the status and gaps in safeguarding structures against interpersonal violence in sports. As a practitioner in sports rehabilitation, you should understand these structural issues and consider how to better identify, report, and support victims in your practice to ensure athlete safety.
items.limitation: 本研究为横断面调查，依赖于自我报告数据，可能存在报告偏差。
items.limitationEn: This is a cross-sectional survey relying on self-reported data, which may introduce reporting bias.

items.title: Towards safer and smarter boxing: technology, health and performance
items.summary: This article discusses the need for technology, health, and performance integration in boxing to move beyond subjective judging and intuition-based training, especially concerning traumatic brain injury management.
items.curatedReason: 这篇述评强调了拳击运动中头部损伤的严重性和现有管理策略的局限性，并呼吁引入技术和数据驱动的方法。这提醒运动康复医师在拳击运动员的伤病管理和预防中应积极探索更科学、更个性化的干预手段。
items.titleZh: 迈向更安全、更智能的拳击：技术、健康与表现
items.summaryZh: 本文讨论了拳击运动中整合技术、健康与表现的必要性，以超越主观评判和基于直觉的训练，尤其是在创伤性脑损伤管理方面。
items.curatedReasonEn: This perspective highlights the severity of head injuries in boxing and the limitations of current management strategies, advocating for technology and data-driven approaches. This reminds sports rehabilitation clinicians to actively explore more scientific and personalized interventions in the injury management and prevention of boxers.
items.limitation: 本研究为一篇述评，不包含原始研究数据，仅代表作者观点。
items.limitationEn: This is a perspective piece, not containing original research data, and represents only the authors' views.

items.title: Figure-of-Eight Taping Combined with Rehabilitation for Multidirectional Shoulder Instability in a School-Age Female Karate Athlete: A Case Report.
items.summary: A case report describes a 12-year-old female karate athlete with multidirectional shoulder instability (MDI) whose exercise therapy was effectively facilitated by figure-of-eight taping, addressing discomfort and pain.
items.curatedReason: 这个病例报告提示，八字贴扎可能为多方向肩关节不稳（MDI）患者，尤其在康复早期，提供额外的支持，帮助其更有效地进行运动治疗。但作为单一病例，证据强度有限，不宜直接推而广之。
items.titleZh: 八字贴扎联合康复治疗学龄期女性空手道运动员多方向肩关节不稳一例报告
items.summaryZh: 一例病例报告描述了一名 12 岁女性空手道运动员，其多方向肩关节不稳（MDI）的运动疗法通过八字贴扎得到有效促进，改善了不适和疼痛。
items.curatedReasonEn: This case report suggests that figure-of-eight taping might offer additional support for individuals with multidirectional shoulder instability (MDI), particularly in early rehabilitation, facilitating more effective exercise therapy. However, as a single case, the evidence strength is limited and should not be generalized.
items.limitation: 本研究为单病例报告，证据等级低，结果外推性差。
items.limitationEn: This is a single case report, with low evidence level and limited generalizability.
items.journal: International journal of sports physical therapy

items.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
items.summary: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions. The review included randomized clinical trials.
items.curatedReason: 低负荷血流限制训练（LLE-BFR）在肌肉骨骼康复中，对改善肌力、减轻残疾和疼痛方面的额外益处已经过系统综述与荟萃分析证实。这意味着你可以考虑在常规低负荷训练中加入血流限制，以期获得更好的临床效果，尤其是在患者不耐受高负荷训练时。
items.titleZh: 血流限制训练在肌肉骨骼康复中是否比常规训练有额外益处？一项系统综述与荟萃分析。
items.summaryZh: 一项系统综述和荟萃分析发现，与低负荷运动（LLE）相比，低负荷血流限制运动（LLE-BFR）在改善肌肉骨骼疾病患者的肌力、残疾和疼痛强度方面具有额外益处。该综述纳入了随机对照试验数据。
items.curatedReasonEn: This systematic review and meta-analysis confirms the additional benefits of low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) in musculoskeletal rehabilitation for improving muscle strength, reducing disability, and alleviating pain. You can consider incorporating BFR into conventional low-load exercise, especially when high-load training is not tolerated, to achieve superior clinical outcomes.
items.limitation: 本研究的局限性在于无法明确区分 LLE-BFR 与高负荷运动 (HLE) 相比的优劣，且未详细报告各结局的具体效应量。
items.limitationEn: The limitation of this study is its inability to clearly differentiate the superiority of LLE-BFR compared to high-load exercise (HLE), and it does not explicitly report specific effect sizes for each outcome.

items.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
items.summary: A randomized controlled trial demonstrated high agreement between physiotherapists, resident physicians, and senior physicians in diagnosing, treating, and discharging musculoskeletal conditions in an emergency department setting. This supports the implementation of Advanced Physiotherapy Practice roles.
items.curatedReason: 在急诊科场景中，物理治疗师（PT）与住院医师在肌肉骨骼功能障碍的诊断、治疗和出院决策方面与资深医师具有高度一致性。这意味着你可以利用这一证据推动高级物理治疗实践（APP）角色在急诊科的部署，减轻急诊压力并优化患者分流。
items.titleZh: 肌肉骨骼功能障碍诊断与治疗中的一线共识：一项前瞻性随机对照研究
items.summaryZh: 一项随机对照试验显示，在急诊科中，物理治疗师与住院医师在肌肉骨骼功能障碍的诊断、治疗和出院决策上与资深医师具有高度一致性。这支持了高级物理治疗实践（APP）角色的实施。
items.curatedReasonEn: In emergency department (ED) settings, physiotherapists (PTs) and resident physicians show high agreement with senior physicians on diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. This evidence supports advancing the deployment of Advanced Physiotherapy Practice (APP) roles in EDs, which can alleviate ED pressure and optimize patient triage.
items.limitation: 本研究是在单一中心进行的小样本随机对照试验，结果的外推性可能受限。
items.limitationEn: This study was a small-sample randomized controlled trial conducted at a single center, which may limit the generalizability of its findings.
